issues.description: All FAQ question buttons contain an empty <span> intended to render a Font Awesome Duotone icon, but the icon character/glyph is missing — the span renders as a blank gap (≈2.3rem wide) next to every question. Users see an unexplained empty box rather than the intended icon.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Vision globale du portefeuille' screenshot
issues.description: The product screenshot used (Portfolio projects kanban status 'en.png') is the English-language UI version, while the entire page is in French. All other feature screenshots appear to use French UI. This creates a visible language inconsistency in a prominent product image.

The nav bar's 'Demander une démo' button links to '/fr/meetings-pages', which is a plain slug that looks like a routing stub rather than the expected booking/calendar page. The hero CTA 'Réservez une démo' also links there — if this slug is broken or wrong, both primary CTAs are dead.

The H1 '80 projets côté DSI. 80 côté Marketing. 80 côté Finance. Et toujours pas de vue commune au Codir ?' is nearly word-for-word repeated as the body copy in the 'Vision globale du portefeuille' feature section further down the page, creating visible redundancy for anyone who scrolls.

All feature sections (Agent IA, Email Bilan de santé, Flash Report, etc.) use H3 tags despite there being no H2 grouping heading above them on this landing page. The page jumps from H1 → (logo band H2) → H3 for individual features, skipping a logical H2 level that would group the feature showcase.

issues.location: Trusted logos section — 'Ils nous font confiance'
issues.description: All five logo images have empty alt attributes (alt=''), which means screen readers get no information about which companies trust AirSaas. Given the section's purpose is social proof by named brands, each logo should have a descriptive alt with the company name.

The Font Awesome icon spans inside each FAQ accordion button render as empty (no visible icon/glyph). The font-family 'Font Awesome 6 Duotone' is being set via inline style but no icon unicode content is placed inside the span, leaving a blank space where expand/collapse indicators should be.

issues.location: Feature sections (Agent IA Brief projet, Agent IA Découpage projet, etc.)
issues.description: All eight alternating feature sections use H3 headings with no H2 parent grouping them. The page jumps from H1 (hero) directly to the trust-logo H2, then into H3 feature sections with no H2 introducing the feature group, creating a broken heading hierarchy.

The secondary CTA 'Découvrir le guide Capacity Planning' links to '/fr/livre-blanc/capacity-planning' which is a different page/resource, but it sits visually peer-level with the primary demo CTA and uses an orange-bright color not part of the stated lavender/primary palette, making it look like a brand outlier rather than an intentional secondary action.

issues.location: Agent IA Découpage projet — feature section body
issues.description: The body copy ends abruptly: 'Elle connaît vos équipes : ce qu'elles savent faire, ce qu'elles ne font pas, leur vélocité passée.' There is no mention of what the agent actually outputs or why it matters for capacity planning — the paragraph reads like a truncated draft missing a closing benefit statement.

FAQ question labels switch font-size between mobile (text-[1.1rem]) and desktop (md:text-[1.6875rem]), a 53% jump. At the desktop size (~27px) this is larger than typical body text but smaller than the section H2, making the FAQ questions appear as an awkward in-between heading size that clashes with the surrounding typographic scale.

The mobile nav button sets aria-expanded='false' and aria-controls='mobile-nav', but there is no element with id='mobile-nav' visible in the rendered HTML, suggesting the mobile drawer panel is either missing or not rendered at all in this LP template.

The 'Ils nous font confiance' section shows only 5 logos (Kiabi, Altavia, Valrhona, Intuis, SNCF) with no client names as text fallback — all images have empty alt attributes — making the section invisible to screen readers and providing zero textual proof of trust signals.

All feature sections use <h3> headings, but the preceding 'Ils nous font confiance' section uses <h2>. There is no <h2> introducing the feature block, so the heading hierarchy jumps from <h1> directly into a trust-logo <h2> and then unexplained <h3>s with no parent <h2> contextualising the feature group — illogical outline for screen readers and SEO.

The secondary CTA '▶️ Découvrir l'outil PPM en vidéo (5 min)' links to '/fr/video/ppm'. This is a dedicated video page that may not exist in the rebuild (no evidence in the HTML of that route), which would produce a 404 for a prominent hero CTA.

Every FAQ toggle button contains an empty <span> intended to render a Font Awesome Duotone icon (font-family set inline), but no icon character/glyph is present — the span is completely empty. Users will see a blank ~37px-wide gap instead of an icon, making the accordion look broken.

The nav CTA 'Demander une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages' while the hero CTA 'Réservez une démo' also links to '/fr/meetings-pages'. Consistent destination is fine, but the nav label says 'Demander' while every other CTA on the page says 'Réservez' — inconsistent verb creates confusion. More critically, this is a landing page (lp/ppm) and the nav retains full site navigation including competitor-adjacent dropdowns, which is atypical for a conversion-focused LP and may distract visitors.

The pricing answer is intentionally vague ('Prix accessible. Tarification sur mesure…') but ends with 'Parlons-en lors d'une démo' without any CTA link or button — the sentence prompts an action the user cannot take from that element, leaving the interaction dead-ended.

The hero section has `min-h-screen` and a large gradient blur at the bottom, but contains no screenshot or product image — it is purely text + two buttons. After the CTAs the page jumps immediately to the logo strip, leaving a visually light hero that may feel unfinished compared to the feature sections below which all have product screenshots.

issues.location: FAQ — first answer ('AirSaas remplace-t-il Jira ou Asana ?')
issues.description: The answer explicitly names competitor tools Jira and Asana in a way that may be intentional, but the phrasing 'Vos équipes gardent Jira/Asana' could unintentionally validate those products as the default operational layer. Worth a brand/copy review to ensure this is the intended positioning statement.

The H1 'Un PPM avec une UX au top ? Ça existe.' uses colloquial language ('au top') that may feel inconsistent with the more formal enterprise tone of the feature copy and FAQ below. This is a minor brand-voice inconsistency worth reviewing for the final ship.

The answer to the pricing question is 'Prix accessible. Parlons-en lors d'une démo.' — this is a non-answer that dodges the question entirely. For a landing page aimed at converting RTEs/Comex stakeholders, this reads as evasive and undermines trust; at minimum it should reference a pricing page or ballpark.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Les objectifs PI ne disparaissent plus' image
issues.description: The image used (Capacity screen.webp — alt='Capacity') does not illustrate PI objective tracking; it appears to be a capacity planning screenshot reused from a different section, making the visual mismatched with the copy about continuous PI goal follow-up.

issues.location: Section 'AirSaas : la couche business au-dessus de Jira'

issues.location: Feature section — 'Capacity' image ("Peut-on prendre ce projet ?")
issues.description: The screenshot (quarter plan teams EN.webp) is labelled as an English-language UI ('EN') on a French landing page. Showing an English UI to French prospects is inconsistent with the rest of the page which uses French screenshots.

The nav CTA links to '/fr/meetings-pages' while the hero CTA also links to '/fr/meetings-pages' — both are consistent, but the hero secondary CTA links to '/fr/video/pi-planning'. If that video page does not exist in the rebuild it will 404; this should be verified before ship.

The heading 'Questions fréquentes' uses two differently-styled gradient spans ('Questions' dark-to-primary, 'fréquentes' primary gradient) which is decoratively fine, but 'Questions fréquentes' is notably plain compared to every other section heading on the page that carries product-specific phrasing — it feels like a generic placeholder title.

The page body contains only two sections: the hero and a single feature block ('Ajoutez les sponsors sur vos projets'), then jumps directly to the footer. The live page has multiple feature sections explaining the health-check email product. The rebuild is missing the vast majority of page content, making this page essentially a stub.

The H1 reads 'Email "bilan de santé" : Automatisez la communication projet' — this uses an H1 for the page title, but the only subsequent content section uses an H3 ('Ajoutez les sponsors sur vos projets'). With so few sections, the heading hierarchy is technically valid but the H3 appears as a top-level feature heading with no H2 parent, creating a skipped level in the outline.

The primary CTA 'Je veux une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages' — this is the same destination used by both the nav 'Demander une démo' button and the hero CTA, which is acceptable, but the label mismatch ('Je veux une démo' vs 'Demander une démo') across the same page is inconsistent and may confuse users. More critically, if the meetings-pages route is the correct demo page it should be verified as live; given the product page context, a more specific CTA label tied to the feature ('Essayer l'email bilan de santé') would be expected.
The footer copyright reads '© 2026 AirSaas' — 2026 is a future year which may look like a typo to visitors reviewing the page today, and could undermine brand credibility.

With only one feature block present before the footer, the page layout looks severely truncated — a lone text+image row followed immediately by the footer creates a jarring visual gap and signals to the user that content failed to load.

The Font Awesome icon spans inside each FAQ accordion button render empty (no glyph visible) because the font is referenced by name ('Font Awesome 6 Duotone') but almost certainly not loaded in the rebuild, leaving a blank ~2.3rem space before every question label.

Every body text section applies `lg:px-[10rem] lg:py-[6.25rem]` — the same large padding used for section breaks — to plain mid-paragraph text blocks, causing excessive vertical whitespace between paragraphs that visually looks like separate standalone sections rather than continuous article prose.

Préparation…`), which is invalid HTML. Browsers will silently break this, likely causing the inner paragraph to render outside its container and producing a visible layout gap between the heading and the content below.

issues.location: Section 'Le pilotage par la valeur' (second section)

The heading hierarchy jumps from H2 directly into bare paragraphs with no H3 to introduce each topic.

issues.location: Section 'Suivi de l'indicateur d'adhésion' — body copy

The hero paragraph text runs without a space between two sentences ('etc.).La mesure'). This produces a word-boundary error visible to readers on screen.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Embarquez par une bonne communication' image alt text
issues.description: The image alt text is 'Pilotage de projet', which is generic and does not describe the image shown (what appears to be a communication/engagement view). This is misleading for screen-reader users and inconsistent with the section heading.

issues.location: Section 'Le pilotage par la valeur' vs following unlabelled body-copy section
issues.description: The rich body text about 'prioriser les investissements' lives in a completely separate <section> with no heading, immediately after the heading-only stub section. ` blocks styled as body copy with no H2/H3 to anchor them. Readers and search engines cannot parse the content hierarchy; these sections appear as one undifferentiated wall of text.

The hero subtitle abruptly splices two distinct ideas mid-paragraph: '…Pour vous aider, nous avons conçu le grand guide de la conduite de projet.Mesurer ses performances…' — there is no line-break or transition between the guide promotion sentence and the next thought, making the paragraph read as two unrelated fragments run together.

The H3 heading says 'Quelques principes à respecter pour une gestion de portefeuille optimale' but its body copy only contains a single introductory sentence ('Il est important de respecter certaines règles…') — the actual bulleted rules appear in a completely separate preceding plain-text section with no heading. The content and its heading are decoupled, which confuses the user.

The heading promises '3 vues' but only one view is described in the body copy ('Un suivi de l'avancée de chacun de vos projets…'). The other two views are never presented, leaving the section visually and semantically incomplete.

issues.location: Unnamed plain-text sections (2nd and 3rd body sections)
issues.description: Several consecutive sections contain body copy that lacks any section heading or visual separator, making it impossible for a user scanning the page to understand what topic each block addresses. These are effectively orphan paragraphs with no label.

The link to 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/gestion-de-projet/kpi-gestion-de-projet' is an absolute URL pointing to the live production domain rather than a relative path, meaning it will leave the rebuild environment and land on the live site — inconsistent with all other internal links on the page which use relative paths.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Tableau de bord de gestion de projets : pourquoi est-ce crucial…'

The hero has only one CTA ('Réservez une démo') with no secondary action (e.g. 'Voir la démo' or 'Essayer gratuitement'), which is thin compared to the rest of the site pattern and may reduce conversion for users not yet ready to book a meeting.

issues.location: Integrations section ("Vos équipes vont adorer nos intégrations natives")
issues.description: The integrations section contains only a heading and a short descriptive paragraph — there are no integration logos, cards, or visuals. The section is functionally empty/stub and offers no actual content to the user.

This section has a heading and a single paragraph of text but no accompanying image or visual — every comparable feature section on the page pairs text with a product screenshot. This section appears truncated/incomplete.

This section uses an H3 tag while it sits at the same structural level as other feature sections that also use H3 — but unlike them, it is styled with the gradient heading treatment normally reserved for H2 section titles, creating an inconsistent visual and semantic hierarchy.

The hero section contains only one CTA button ("Réservez une démo") with no secondary CTA (e.g. free trial or product tour link), whereas comparable solution pages typically offer a secondary action. This is a conversion gap for this high-intent audience page.

"Ils parlent de nous" ("They talk about us") is a press/media logos strip, but the heading copy implies customer testimonials rather than press mentions. A heading like "Ils nous font confiance" or "La presse en parle" would better match the media-logo content.

issues.location: Feature section heading — "Obtenez la bonne information , au bon moment"
issues.description: There is a stray space before the comma in "information , au bon moment", producing a typographic error visible in the rendered heading.

element (wrapped in a ), which is invalid HTML. Browsers will auto-correct this by splitting elements, potentially breaking the intended layout or losing content.

However the 'Uniformisez' section is a standalone text-only section with no image and no H2 parent — it floats between two image-paired H3 sections as if it were a sub-heading or intro, but it is styled identically, making the heading hierarchy and content grouping ambiguous and visually broken.

The section heading 'Ils parlent de nous' (They talk about us) describes press/media coverage, but the section only contains media logo images with no accompanying quotes, article titles, or links. This is a stub press-mention section with no actionable content — a human reviewer would expect at least a quote or a link to validate the claim.

The hero paragraph ends with 'Avec AirSaas, positionnez vous au centre de cette transformation.' — 'positionnez vous' should be hyphenated as 'positionnez-vous' in standard French (missing hyphen in imperative reflexive construction), which is a minor but visible grammatical error on the most prominent section of the page.

The heading 'Ils parlent de nous' (They talk about us) is used for a press/media logos section, but this label implies customer testimonials. The correct label should be something like 'Ils écrivent sur nous' or 'Ils en parlent dans la presse'. This is a semantic mismatch that could mislead visitors.
The heading contains a typo: 'marketplate' instead of 'marketplace'. This is a visible brand/typo error in a prominent H2.

issues.location: Section 'La plateforme qui fluidifie votre gouvernance projet' and 'La marketplate AirSaas'

issues.location: Section 'Laissez nos clients vous parler d'AirSaas'

issues.location: Section: 'Laissez nos clients vous parler d'AirSaas'
issues.description: Testimonials section renders only a heading and an intro paragraph — zero testimonial cards are present. The entire social proof content is missing, making this a visible empty stub.

issues.location: Section: 'Animez clairement et simplement vos CoPil'

issues.location: Sections: governance, capacity planning, marketplace, testimonials
issues.description: Four consecutive sections use H2 as standalone heading-only blocks with only a short intro paragraph and no sub-content — this creates an awkward wall of H2 headings with no visual content between them, appearing broken even though HTML is valid.

'Et oui un PPM peut avoir une UX au top' reads as an informal aside that feels tonally inconsistent mid-paragraph; while not blocking, it appears to be a stray editorial note that was likely meant to be removed or styled differently.

The word 'traitchef ement' appears in the body copy ('L'identification des risques et leur traitchef ement doit se faire…'). This is a corrupted word — likely 'traitement' with a stray 'chef' inserted — and is visibly broken to any reader.

The article body opens directly with an H3 ('Pourquoi est-ce important en gestion de projet ?') as the first structural heading under the H1 page title. There is no H2 wrapping the article body content, so the heading hierarchy jumps from H1 → H3, which is semantically broken and bad for screen readers and SEO.

issues.description: The link to the 'conduite de projet' guide uses a relative URL '/gestion-de-projet/le-grand-guide-de-la-conduite-de-projet' (no locale prefix), while the page is under '/fr/'. This will 404 or redirect incorrectly for French-locale users.

issues.description: Each bullet in the risk-type list is wrapped in its own separate <ul> element instead of a single <ul> with multiple <li> children. While visually similar, this produces semantically fragmented markup and may cause inconsistent spacing or screen-reader enumeration issues.

issues.location: Blog article body — figcaption under AirSaas screenshot
issues.description: The figcaption reads 'Penser autrement sa mise en mise en œuvre de la gestion des risques' — 'mise en mise en œuvre' is a duplicated phrase fragment. This is a source content issue that is visible to users and should be fixed.

The opening paragraph ends mid-thought with an unclosed quotation: 'Non on y va pas, c'est trop risqué ! On est à N % de risques / On time / On cost / on quality !' The 'N %' placeholder and the unclosed rhetorical question make the intro look like stub/template copy that was never finalised.

issues.location: Blog body — Idée reçue n°4, fourth paragraph
issues.description: The paragraph beginning 'Ensuite, qu'on définit le troisième workshop…' is grammatically broken and appears truncated mid-thought ('Déjà,' ends the paragraph with no conclusion). This reads as raw notes rather than finished copy.

issues.location: Blog body — Idée reçue n°4, quote about community

issues.location: Blog body — L'essentiel en quelques mots, second paragraph

The article introduction promises 'neuf étapes d'un appel d'offres' but the visible HTML only contains the 'Sept idées reçues' section and a 'Conclusions' anchor in the table of contents — the nine-steps section appears to be entirely missing from the rebuild.

The first body section 'L'essentiel en quelques mots…' uses an H3, but this is the top-level section of the article body (below the H1 hero title). It should be H2 to maintain a correct heading hierarchy; H3 implies a sub-section of an H2 that doesn't exist.

The article is attributed 'Le 1 février 2026' which is a future date at time of rebuild. While the system prompt notes future dates are not inherently a fabrication flag, this specific date is suspiciously far out and may indicate a CMS field was not correctly mapped during migration.

issues.location: Blog body — Idée reçue n°3, paragraph mentioning competitors
issues.description: The text calls out competitor tools by name ('JIRA Asana ou Wrike') in a way that could be intentional positioning, but reads as recommending rival products without context — worth a content review to confirm this was intentional and not stray copy.

The sommaire lists only three entries (L'essentiel, Sept idées reçues, Conclusions) but the article text promises nine detailed steps section. The missing section means one or more anchor links in the TOC will lead to non-existent targets or the TOC is incomplete.

issues.location: Retour d'expérience du CIO d'Adeo — callout aside
issues.description: The 'À retenir' blockquote is truncated mid-sentence: 'Au niveau budget, l'idée, c'est de fonctionner sur enveloppe fixe. Et moi, je cape l'investissement général, d'Adeo dans le digital, par rapport à la croissance de l'entreprise. On' — the quote cuts off abruptly after 'On' with no continuation, leaving an incomplete sentence visible to users.

The link to 'conduite de projet' points to '/gestion-de-projet/le-grand-guide-de-la-conduite-de-projet' (relative, no locale prefix), which will resolve incorrectly under the /fr/ path structure used by the rebuild. It should be '/fr/gestion-de-projet/le-grand-guide-de-la-conduite-de-projet' or the canonical blog equivalent.

The text reads 'le spécialiste français et coach agile Frédéric' — the full name is truncated (surname missing). This appears to be a CMS rendering issue where only the first name was pulled, leaving an incomplete attribution that looks like a bug to readers.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026' which is a future date relative to typical publication cadence for this content. While the system prompt notes future dates are not auto-flagged as fabricated, the format 'Le 1 février' is grammatically incorrect in French — it should be 'Le 1er février' (ordinal required for the first day of the month).

The CTA button is labelled 'Télécharger' but links to '/fr/meetings-pages' (a demo/meetings page). The label does not match the destination or the surrounding content context about collaboration planning, making it misleading and likely to erode trust.

issues.location: Body content – bullet list on resource types
issues.description: The list of resource types managed in capacity planning contains only two items ('Les ressources financières' and 'Les ressources humaines') but the following sentence refers to 'ce dernier point' implying at least a third category (e.g. material/technical resources) is missing — the list appears truncated from the source.
The section body is truncated mid-sentence ('nous avons') in the rendered HTML. The product pitch and any closing CTA for AirSaas are cut off, leaving an incomplete paragraph visible to the reader.

issues.location: Body – 'Ne vous y détrompez pas' paragraph
issues.description: 'Ne vous y détrompez pas' is a grammatical error in French; the correct idiom is 'Ne vous y trompez pas'. This is a content/copy bug that appears directly in the rebuild output and is user-visible.

The sommaire lists only 3 top-level entries but the article body contains 8 named sub-sections (h4 headings). None of the 8 individual best-practice subsections are linked from the table of contents, making the TOC incomplete and potentially confusing for readers expecting direct navigation.

The link to the previous article uses 'http://www.airsaas.io' (HTTP, non-canonical, with www) instead of 'https://www.airsaas.io'. Mixed-content or redirect issues may occur; all internal links should use the canonical HTTPS origin.

issues.location: Body – list item 'Être prêt à accueillir une variation de la demande'
issues.description: Missing space before 'sur une période donnée' — the rendered text reads '…variation de la demande·sur une période donnée' with no space after the bold closing tag. This is a visible typographic glitch in the running text.

The author avatar image URL loads a photo labelled 'Avatar Jérôme' (from the CDN filename 'Avatar Jérôme.png') but the displayed name is 'Jonas Roman'. The image and the name do not match the same person, which is a visible credibility issue.

issues.location: Blog body — first content section heading
issues.description: The first body section ('Qu'est-ce que la gestion de portefeuille de projets ?') is rendered as an <h3> rather than an <h2>. The page has an H1 (article title) and then jumps directly to H3, skipping H2 entirely, breaking the heading hierarchy and harming accessibility/SEO.

issues.location: Blog body — 'Ce que la gestion de portefeuille de projets permet' paragraph
issues.description:
The sentence 'Ce que la gestion de portefeuille de projets permet' is rendered as a plain

tag instead of a heading or labeled list title. It reads like a truncated or unstyled heading sitting awkwardly before the bullet list, giving the impression the content was cut off mid-structure.

The first comparison table (Gestion des ressources vs Planification de la capacité) has an empty <th> cell in the first column header position, leaving the row label column visually unlabeled. This is confusing for readers scanning the table.

The 'Sommaire' heading uses var(--text-h2) with gradient clip text styling, which is the full marketing-section treatment. For a utilitarian table-of-contents block inside an article, this large gradient heading is visually disproportionate and inconsistent with standard blog article TOC styling.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026', which is a future date far beyond any plausible authoring date for this evergreen PMO article. Unlike minor date discrepancies, this date is over a year ahead and will confuse readers who see it as an obvious error.

The article body jumps directly from H1 (page title) to H3 for all section headings (e.g., 'L'envolée des projets transverses', 'Projets silos et transformation'…) with no H2 intermediate level. The table of contents is in an H2, but the body sections skip H2 entirely, creating an illogical and broken heading hierarchy for screen readers and SEO.

issues.location: Blog body — internal link in body text
issues.description: The link to the PMO métier article points to 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/gestion-de-projet/metier-pmo' (the live domain with the old URL structure), not a relative rebuild path. This is a hardcoded absolute URL to the production site embedded inside the rebuilt page body, which will break navigation on the rebuild environment.

issues.location: Blog body — paragraph with zero-width space
issues.description: A paragraph containing only a '‍' (zero-width joiner / empty spacer) is rendered as a visible empty paragraph block between sections. This is a Webflow CMS artefact that should be stripped in the rebuild rather than passed through to the DOM.

issues.location: Table of contents — last entry anchor
issues.description: The last TOC entry links to '#quel-est-le-bon-moment-quelle-est-la-bonne-taille-dentreprise-pour-mettre-en-pla' — the anchor ID is visibly truncated mid-word ('pla'). If the body section ID is generated differently, this link will be dead and the anchor will not scroll correctly.

The body cites 'études Mc Kinsey' (with a space in McKinsey). While this may be a faithful copy from the source CMS, it is a misspelling of the brand name 'McKinsey' that is user-visible and reflects poorly on brand credibility.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026', which is a future date well beyond any plausible review window. This is likely a data-entry error (2026 instead of 2022) that will confuse readers and hurt credibility.

The link to '/gestion-de-projet/copil-projet-ou-comite-de-pilotage-projet-les-bases' is a relative path missing the locale prefix '/fr/', which will 404 on the rebuilt site where all routes are under /fr/.

issues.location: Blog body — all absolute internal links
issues.description: Several in-body links point to 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/...' (e.g. comite-de-pilotage-definitions-et-incomprehensions, comment-decider-en-copil, chef-de-projet-transverse). These hardcode the live domain instead of using relative paths, so clicks from the rebuild environment will leave the rebuilt site entirely.

issues.location: Blog body — paragraph with empty strong tag
issues.description: The paragraph 'Mettez en place les bons outilspour récolter...' contains an empty tag acting as a spacing hack, producing a missing space between 'outils' and 'pour' in rendered output.

issues.location: Blog body — heading 'Avoir rassemblé toutes les informations nécessaires à la prises de décision'
issues.description: 'à la prises de décision' is a grammatical error (should be 'à la prise de décision'). While this originates in the source CMS, it is user-visible and worth correcting before ship.

issues.location: Blog body — heading 'Planifier le Copil longtemps à l'avance'
issues.description: This H4 is immediately followed by an image block and then another H4 ('S'assurer de la présence…') with no body text between them, making the 'Planifier le Copil' sub-section appear empty/stub to the reader.

issues.location: Blog body — paragraph ending '…et instaurer les principes de gouvernance.Cette grille'
issues.description: There is no space between the closing bold period and 'Cette', causing 'gouvernance.Cette' to render as a run-together word in the browser.

The first 'À retenir' callout text is truncated mid-sentence: '…Sauf qu'on fait 500 millions de C' — the sentence is cut off and the rest of the content is missing, leaving a broken quote visible to users.

issues.location: À retenir callout — 'L'illusion du pilotage stratégique' section
issues.description: The second 'À retenir' callout is also truncated mid-sentence: '…Trois semaines dans une organisation de 2 100 personnes. Chief' — the speaker's title and organisation are cut off, making the quote incomplete and unprofessional.

issues.location: À retenir callout — 'Le mal invisible des COMEX' section (and likely further callouts)
issues.description: The HTML truncation note shows the pattern continues ('Nous partageons la même stratégie, mais no…'), strongly indicating multiple additional callout quotes are also cut mid-sentence throughout the article body.

issues.location: Blog article body — all section headings
issues.description: All article section headings use <h3> while the page's only <h1> is the article title and there is no <h2> bridging structure in the body. The TOC section uses an <h2> ('Sommaire') but the article content jumps directly to <h3> headings, creating a skipped heading level (h1 → h3) that breaks both accessibility and SEO heading hierarchy.

issues.location: Hero — article title vs. URL slug
issues.description: The page slug is 'pourquoi-vos-18-millions' and the original live URL references '18 millions', but the rendered H1 reads 'Pourquoi vos millions de transformation s'évaporent' (without '18'). The number '18' also appears in section anchor IDs and body copy but is absent from the H1, creating an inconsistency that may confuse readers arriving via the titled URL.

issues.location: À retenir callout — 'L'illusion du pilotage stratégique' section (quote attribution)
issues.description: The quote 'Je croyais avoir la main sur nos transformations…' is attributed to 'DG, groupe technologique, 1 400 salariés' — this looks like a pulled quote that should have a distinct visual treatment (blockquote / testimonial card) rather than being inside an 'À retenir' info-box, conflating editorial insight with a named executive quote.

issues.location: À retenir callout — 'CRM, finance, data…' section
issues.description: The callout quote 'Nous gérons nos projets stratégiques avec les mêmes méthodes qu'en 2004…' is attributed inline as 'COO, ETI de service' without any formatting distinction from the surrounding 'À retenir' editorial notes, making it unclear whether this is a client testimonial or an editorial statement.

Several TOC anchor hrefs contain accented characters (e.g. '#lillusion-du-pilotage-stratégique', '#crm-finance-data-pourquoi-la-gouvernance-projet-est-restée-à-lâge-des-powerpoint') which may fail to resolve correctly in some browsers if the corresponding heading IDs are not identically encoded, risking broken in-page navigation.

issues.location: First 'À retenir' callout (before H3 'Qu'est-ce qu'un PI Planning ?')
issues.description: The first expert quote callout is visibly truncated mid-sentence: 'Ils apportent aussi leurs bonnes pratiques' — the sentence ends abruptly with no closing punctuation or continuation. This appears before any section anchor, making it the first substantive callout a reader encounters.

issues.location: Second 'À retenir' callout (after first H3 definition paragraph)
issues.description: Expert quote is truncated mid-sentence: 'c'est que les gens qui y participent sont ceux qui y mett' — the word 'mettent' (or similar) is cut off. The CMS excerpt is rendering incomplete text rather than the full quote.

issues.location: Third 'À retenir' callout (H4 'PI Planning et framework SAFe')
issues.description: Expert quote ends abruptly: 'Les valeurs temporelles comme le trimestre sont très a' — clearly truncated. Multiple callouts sharing this truncation pattern suggests the expert-quote component is systematically cutting off the content field at a character limit.

issues.location: Fourth 'À retenir' callout (after quarterly rhythm paragraph)
issues.description: Expert quote is cut mid-sentence: 'dans un contexte de gestion de portefeuille de projets, il peut' — same truncation bug. All five 'À retenir' callouts in the visible portion appear to suffer from this, indicating a systemic data-fetching or rendering issue with the expert quote field.

issues.location: Fifth 'À retenir' callout (under H4 'Quels sont les bénéfices')
issues.description: Expert quote ends abruptly: 'les capacités comme le budget à attribuer' — sentence is unfinished. Confirms the systemic truncation of all expert-quote callouts on the page; the rendered output is missing the full CMS content.

The anchor '#à-quoi-ressemble-un-pi-planning-réussi-avec-des-bénéfices-concrets' and '#dès-sa-préparation-faites-de-votre-pi-planning-un-atout-pour-transformer' contain raw accented characters in href values; these should be percent-encoded (e.g. '%C3%A0') to ensure reliable cross-browser navigation without JS smoothScroll fallback.

The article opens with H3 ('Qu'est-ce qu'un PI Planning ?') as the first content heading under the H1/H2 hero and sommaire. There is no H2 wrapping the article body, creating a gap (H1 → H3) that is illogical for accessibility and SEO even accounting for the intentional blog body downshift rule.

The article links to '/fr/gestion-de-projet/pi-safe' (live site path), but the rebuild is served under '/fr/blog/…'. If the rebuild remaps all blog URLs to '/fr/blog/', this internal cross-link still points to the old route and will 404 in the rebuilt environment.

issues.location: Blog body — internal link after section 2
issues.description: The link to the related article uses a relative path without the '/fr/' locale prefix: href='/gestion-de-projet/trois-innovations-et-tendances-qui-bousculent-la-gestion-de-portefeuille-de-projets'. This will 404 on the rebuilt site which uses /fr/ prefixed routes.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026', which is a future date relative to any reasonable review window. This looks like a CMS data error (year should likely be 2023 or 2024 given the article content and image URLs), and will appear as a fabricated or broken date to readers.

issues.location: Blog body — section 1, paragraph about ERP
issues.description: The sentence 'Le programme ERP, SI Finance, ERP ne fera jamais rêver !' repeats 'ERP' twice in the same short phrase ('Le programme ERP, SI Finance, ERP'), which reads as a copy/paste error in the source content that should be corrected (e.g. 'SI Finance, ERP').

The article body sections (1–10) use H3 while the Sommaire section uses H2 — this is noted as intentional for blog body, but the intro content before the first H3 has no section-level heading at all, meaning the article jumps from H1 straight into body paragraphs with no H2 framing the body, which could confuse screen readers and SEO crawlers.

The TOC link for item 7 uses a truncated anchor: '#7-nattendez-pas-dêtre-mature-en-gestion-de-projet-pour-moderniser-votre-gouverna'. If the corresponding H3's auto-generated id is the full string, the truncated href will fail to scroll to the section.

Similarly, TOC item 9 uses a truncated anchor '#9-cultivez-la-transparence-pour-permettre-à-chacun-de-sinformer-efficacement-sur', which may not match the full id generated from the H3 text, breaking in-page navigation.

The article's category breadcrumb links to '/fr/blog/articles' labeled 'Gestion de projet', but the live site uses a dedicated category URL. If the rebuild's /fr/blog/articles route is a generic listing page rather than the 'Gestion de projet' category, the label is misleading.
Paracelse (Paracelsus) is described as 'médecin et alchimiste Russe' — he was Swiss-German, not Russian. This is a factual error visible to readers.
The phrase reads 'L'importance du design n'est plus à démonter' — the correct word is 'démontrer'. 'Démonter' means to dismantle, not to demonstrate.

The article body starts directly with H3 headings (no H2 wrapping the main content sections), while the Sommaire section above uses an H2. The first content heading 'Qu'est-ce qu'un reporting projet…' is an H3, creating a broken hierarchy where H3 appears as the top-level heading in the article body.
'La vue chronologique vous offrent une grande flexibilité' uses a plural verb ('offrent') with a singular subject ('La vue chronologique'). Should be 'vous offre'.

issues.location: Blog article body — Question 5 intro paragraph
issues.description: The phrase 'à l'opposé des démarches data driven excessives, le reporting minimaliste a le vent en poupe' appears after a Paracelse quote but there is no connecting sentence explaining the relevance of the quote to this point — the transition feels abrupt and incomplete.
'autant pour faciliter la collecter que pour développer la compréhension' — 'collecter' is a verb used as a noun here. Should be 'la collecte'.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026' which is a future date at time of most reviews. While the prompt notes future dates are not automatically flagged as fabricated, the date format is also incorrect French — it should be 'Le 1er février 2026' (ordinal 'er' missing).

issues.location: Blog article body — Question 4 list intro
issues.description: The sentence 'En exemple voici un : Contenu type de reporting pour C Level' is grammatically awkward and reads like unformatted CMS content — likely should be 'En exemple, voici un contenu type de reporting pour C-Level :' or similar.

The article is dated 'Le 1 février 2026' which is over two years after the event (Agile en Seine 2023). This is almost certainly a data-mapping or CMS migration bug producing a wrong date, not an intentional future date — the live site shows a 2023/2024 date for this post.
Missing space before the LinkedIn link: 'l'équipe Agile en Seinepour l'organisation' — 'Seinepour' runs together without a space, making the sentence read incorrectly on screen.

The page uses H3 directly for top-level conference sections and H4 for sub-sections, but there is no H2 in the article body (only the 'Sommaire' section uses an H2). The article content skips H2 entirely, creating an illogical heading hierarchy (H1 → H3 → H4) that breaks accessibility and SEO.

issues.location: Blog body — RATP section, image block
issues.description: After the RATP slide image there is a paragraph containing only a zero-width joiner '‍' (rendered as an empty line). This orphan paragraph is a Webflow artifact that survives as visible whitespace and looks like a content gap to the reader.

issues.location: Blog body — PMU section, image block
issues.description: Same zero-width joiner paragraph '‍' appears again after the Nelson Dufossé photo, producing a spurious blank line inside the article flow.

issues.location: Blog body — PMU section, recruitment paragraph
issues.description: Typo in the body copy: 'd'ête un bon développeur' should be 'd'être un bon développeur' — the verb 'être' is misspelled, which is user-visible on a public page.

The first TOC anchor href '#keynote-douverture-julia-de-funès-sur-le-thème-sens-et-responsabilité-en-temps-d' appears truncated (ends mid-word '…en-temps-d'). If the matching section id is not identically truncated, this anchor will silently fail to scroll.
Copy contains 'sur sur toutes les lèvres' (word 'sur' duplicated). This double-word typo appears in the user-visible article body.

The article body opens directly with H3 tags ('Les symptômes d'un PMO dysfonctionnel', etc.) without an H2 parent, creating an illogical heading hierarchy (H1 → H3 → H4). The Sommaire section uses H2 for 'Sommaire' but the article content never uses H2, leaving the body structurally orphaned under the H1.

The article shows 'Le 1 février 2026', which is a future date relative to any plausible review/ship date in 2024–2025, suggesting a CMS data entry error (year digit off by one). This will appear wrong to every reader who sees it.

The anchor linking to 'reporting' points to the absolute URL 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/gestion-de-projet/reporting-pmo' (live domain) instead of a relative path, meaning it will always route users away from localhost/staging to production — a broken cross-environment link.

issues.location: Blog body — internal link 'chefs de projet'
issues.description: The anchor for 'chefs de projet' also uses the absolute live-domain URL 'https://www.airsaas.io/fr/gestion-de-projet/chef-de-projet-pmo' rather than a relative path, same cross-environment routing issue as above.

issues.location: Blog body — paragraph with zero-width joiner
issues.description: There is a standalone paragraph containing only '‍' (a zero-width joiner character) rendered as an empty spacer paragraph between the first figure and the H4 'Incompréhensions et frustrations'. This is a Webflow artifact that should be stripped; it produces an invisible but semantically meaningless DOM node.

The author byline reads 'Publié par' on one line and the author badge on the next, but there is no date label — the date is presented without a 'Le' prefix label row, making the information hierarchy inconsistent (author has a label, date does not share a unified metadata block).

The sommaire link 'Les symptômes d'un PMO dysfonctionnel' uses the href '#les-symptômes-dun-pmo-dysfonctionnel' with an accented character (ô) in the fragment. Most browsers normalise this, but the matching H3 id (if generated from the text) must be identical; a mismatch would silently break in-page navigation.

The link text is just 'reporting' in lowercase mid-sentence, linking to AirSaas's own PMO reporting article, but the surrounding sentence ('Il fait de son rôle une priorité quand il s'agit de fournir beaucoup de [reporting]') makes the link feel like an arbitrary keyword link rather than a meaningful CTA, weakening brand content quality.

The single bullet point inside the 'À retenir' aside is visibly truncated mid-sentence: 'Quand on bascule dans le digital, on doit forcément faire attention au parcours utilisateur. L'ergonomie est beaucoup plus importante que dans un système SAP. On va s'intéresser en' — the text ends abruptly with no conclusion, which is user-visible and reads as a broken page.

The article displays 'Le 1 février 2026', which is more than a year in the future relative to the article's evident 2022 publish date (image URLs contain '6330…' Webflow CMS IDs consistent with late 2022). This looks like a date mapping bug in the rebuild rather than a legitimate future publish date.

The first section heading in the body is an H3 ('PPM : trois innovations qui font la différence') with no preceding H2, meaning the heading hierarchy jumps from H1 (hero title) directly to H3, then to H4 sub-headings. This is an illogical hierarchy beyond the accepted blog-body H3 downshift pattern.

The caption below the KISS-principle image reads 'L'un des meilleurs conseils produigué :-)' — 'produigué' is a clear typo (should be 'prodigué'). This is a user-visible spelling error in rendered body copy.

issues.location: Blog body — figure caption for robots illustration
issues.description: The caption reads 'Accélération digitale / Transformation et changement sont le quotidient des organisations' — 'quotidient' is a misspelling of 'quotidien', making this grammatically incorrect in a user-visible caption.

The brand name is rendered inconsistently throughout the article body: 'Airsaas' (lowercase 's') appears in the hub image alt text and figcaption alongside correct 'AirSaas' usages, creating brand inconsistency visible to readers.

issues.location: Blog body — list item spacing (missing spaces around <strong> tags)
issues.description: Several inline list items render concatenated words due to missing spaces adjacent to <strong> tags (e.g. 'L'usage des API'pour une'interconnexion', 'liste des projets'avec leurs attributs'). While partially a source-content issue, the result is broken French readable by users.

issues.location: Blog article body — 'Description du projet et des livrables inclus' paragraph
issues.description: The word 'quantité' is repeated twice in the same sentence ('la quantité, la quantité, la longueur…'), which reads as a copy-paste error in the source that should have been 'la qualité, la quantité' or similar. This is a visible editorial bug in the rendered page.

The author is labelled 'Jérôme Dard' but the avatar image URL references 'SV-min.jpg', suggesting the photo belongs to a different person (likely the initials S.V.). Author name and photo are mismatched.

issues.location: Blog article body — internal link 'conduite de projet'
issues.description: The link href is '/gestion-de-projet/le-grand-guide-de-la-conduite-de-projet' (missing the '/fr/' locale prefix used everywhere else on the page), which will likely 404 or redirect incorrectly in the rebuilt Next.js routing.

The budget link href is 'https://www.airsaas.io/gestion-de-projet/budgetiser-un-projet-sans-se-louper' — it points to the live production domain instead of a relative internal path, breaking locale consistency and bypassing the rebuild entirely.
The phrase 'les étapes indispensablesest de réaliser' is missing a space before 'est', making it read 'indispensablesest'. This is a missing-space rendering artifact that is visible to readers.

Multiple empty filler paragraphs containing only '‍' (zero-width joiner / Webflow spacer) are rendered as blank paragraph elements throughout the article body, creating invisible but DOM-present noise that could affect spacing and screen-reader output.

issues.location: Blog article body — multiple internal links (e.g. 'l'organisation macro', 'analyse des risques', 'jalons du projet')
issues.description: Several internal links use absolute 'https://www.airsaas.io/...' URLs without '/fr/' locale prefix rather than relative paths, which bypasses the rebuild and could break locale routing or analytics tracking.

The 'Sommaire' heading uses the H2 gradient style (large, centred, gradient text) which is visually dominant for what is essentially a navigation widget — it draws more visual weight than the article H1 context warrants and may feel like a section heading rather than a table-of-contents label.
